summary: All steps completed in a single iteration. Fixed mixed fan-in early-cutoff bug in both edit_param (sequential, lib.rs) and ConcurrentEvalAdapter (concurrent_eval.rs). Sequential fix uses has_changed_parent HashSet seeded from changed param's dependents; concurrent fix uses SkipState struct behind a Mutex for atomic check-and-modify. Added 6 tests: 2 reproducing the bug (sequential + concurrent), 2 triple fan-in variants, 1 freshness postcondition check, 1 regression guard for valid early cutoff. All 161+ tests pass.

All 10 steps completed. Gap 1 (ConcurrentEditResult discards resolution metadata): added resolved_params and diagnostics fields to ConcurrentEditResult, wired resolve_concurrent_edit return values into the result. Gap 2 (no concurrent constraint checking): factored constraint logic into Engine::check_constraints_with_values helper, created edit_check_concurrent function combining concurrent edit + constraint checking. Gap 3 (stale comment): removed 'Currently FAILS' note. Added 5 new tests: resolved_params flow, infeasible diagnostics flow, constraint satisfaction, constraint transitions, and end-to-end resolution+constraints. All 260 tests pass.

description: Fixed clippy::let_and_return lint in SequencedMockConstraintSolver::solve() by returning the if-expression directly instead of via intermediate let binding.

summary: All 4 remaining steps completed. Gap 4 (constraint label regression): added label field to ConstraintNodeData, populated it from CompiledConstraint.label in the graph builder, updated check_constraints_with_values to use cnode.label.clone() instead of hardcoded None. Updated all ConstraintNodeData construction sites in graph.rs tests, demand.rs, and deps.rs. Added 2 tests: edit_check_preserves_constraint_labels (sequential path, initially failing) and edit_check_concurrent_preserves_constraint_labels (concurrent path, passes immediately). All 495 tests pass across the workspace.

All 3 prerequisites and 20 steps completed. Implemented: (1) reify-stdlib eval_builtin with 21 math functions (abs, sqrt, min, max, floor, ceil, round, sign, log, log10, exp, pow, sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, sinh, cosh, tanh) with proper type dispatch for Int/Real/Scalar/Angle. (2) Wired reify-expr FunctionCall evaluation to dispatch through stdlib with strict Undef propagation. (3) Compiled import declarations into CompiledModule.imports with warning diagnostics. (4) Compiled sub-structure declarations into TopologyTemplate.sub_components with arg expression compilation via parent scope. (5) Added sub_component() and import() builder methods. Added 31+ new tests across stdlib, expr, and compiler. All workspace tests pass.

All 4 remaining steps completed (NaN/inf domain-error hardening). Step-21: wrote 7 failing tests for domain errors (sqrt(-1), log(0), log(-1), log10(0), log10(-1), exp(1000), pow(-2,0.5)) — all correctly failed showing NaN/inf being returned instead of Undef. Step-22: added sanitize_value helper that converts non-finite f64 in Value::Real and Value::Scalar to Undef, applied in unary_f64 and binary_f64 — all 7 tests now pass plus 22 existing tests. Step-23: wrote 8 more tests for asin/acos out-of-range, sinh/cosh overflow, and boundary valid inputs — all pass. Step-24: full workspace test suite passes with 0 failures. This completes all 24 steps of the plan.

All 4 steps completed. Step-1: wrote 4 failing tests for DimensionVector::root() (area→length, length^4→length^2, length→fractional, dimensionless→dimensionless). Step-2: implemented root(n) method that divides each Rational exponent by n via Rational::new(num, den*n). Step-3: wrote 4 failing tests for dimension-aware sqrt in stdlib (scalar area, scalar length^4, fractional exponent, negative scalar). Step-4: replaced sqrt from unary_f64 to unary with Scalar pattern match using dimension.root(2), fallback to as_f64 for Int/Real. All workspace tests pass with 0 failures.

All prerequisite and 6 steps completed. Pre-1: added #[doc(hidden)] poison helper methods to ConcurrentEvalAdapter (poison_skip_state, poison_results, poison_values, poison_snapshot_values) — used #[doc(hidden)] instead of #[cfg(test)] since integration tests need access. Steps 1-2: wrote 4 failing tests for accessor poison recovery (is_dirty, values, take_results, skipped), then fixed 4 .unwrap() calls to .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()). Steps 3-4: wrote failing test for evaluate() with poisoned values lock, then fixed 5 .unwrap() calls in evaluate(). Steps 5-6: wrote failing test for SharedPriorityPromoter with poisoned inner Mutex (tests all 6 operations), then fixed all 6 .unwrap() calls. All workspace tests pass with 0 failures. Total: 15 .unwrap() calls on lock acquisitions replaced with consistent PoisonError recovery pattern.

Completed steps 7-8 (post-review feature gating). Step-7: added test-utils feature to Cargo.toml, replaced #[doc(hidden)] with #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-utils"))] on poison helper impl block, wrapped 5 integration tests in #[cfg(feature = "test-utils")] module. Step-8: verified (1) cargo check without feature compiles (poison methods excluded from production API), (2) cargo test --features test-utils passes all 23 integration tests + 73 unit tests, (3) cargo test without feature passes 18 integration tests + 73 unit tests (poison tests correctly excluded). This completes all 8 steps of the plan.

All 1 prerequisite and 8 steps completed. Fixed OCCT kernel unwrap panics on null shape handles: (1) Added #[cfg(test)] insert_null_shape helper using cxx::UniquePtr::null(). (2) Fixed get_shape() two-step error handling: missing key → InvalidReference, null ptr → OperationFailed. (3) Fixed warm_state() with let-else guard to skip null shapes. (4) Added parameter validation (> 0) for Box/Cylinder/Sphere/Fillet dimensions before FFI calls. (5) Added integration test proving kernel thread survives multiple error types. Total: 48 tests pass (9 new), 0 failures.

Completed final 2 steps (NaN/infinity parameter validation). Step-9: wrote 9 failing tests for NaN, +infinity, -infinity across Box/Cylinder/Sphere/Fillet — 6 correctly failed (NaN and +inf slip through x <= 0.0), 3 already passed (-inf caught by <= 0.0, fillet NaN/inf caught by OCCT FFI). Step-10: replaced all x <= 0.0 guards with \!(x.is_finite() && x > 0.0) — all 57 tests pass (9 new + 48 existing). This completes all 10 steps of the plan.

All 2 prerequisites and 8 steps completed. Pre-1: added minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration rules to grammar.js following constraint_declaration pattern. Pre-2: regenerated tree-sitter parser. Step-1: wrote 2 failing parse tests for minimize/maximize. Step-2: added MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl AST types, MemberDecl variants, lower_minimize()/lower_maximize() methods, updated all exhaustive matches. Step-3: added 3 parse validation tests (complex expr, mixed members, span/hash). Step-4: wrote failing compile test for objective field. Step-5: added objective field to TopologyTemplate, compiled minimize/maximize into OptimizationObjective, added builder method. Step-6: added maximize compile test and backward compatibility test. Step-7: added E2E round-trip integration test. Step-8: fixed boundary1_producer.rs exhaustive match, all 618 workspace tests pass with 0 regressions.

All 1 prerequisite and 30 steps completed in a single iteration. Extended Value enum with 5 new variants (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option) with content_hash, PartialEq, PartialOrd/Ord for each. Extended Type enum with 6 new variants (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option, Function). Added 7 trait definition types in traits.rs (EnumDef, PortDirection, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, TraitMember, TraitDef). Updated format_value in reify-lsp for new variants. Added Ord derives to Rational/DimensionVector. Fixed exhaustive match in reify-test-support builders.rs. Total: 148 tests pass across unit + integration.

Create the new traits.rs module file and register it in lib.rs. This is a new file for EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection.; Write failing tests for Value::Ord implementation: assert Undef < Bool(false) < Bool(true) < Int(0) < Real(0.0) < Scalar < String < Enum < List < Set < Map < Option. Test that values of the same type sort correctly within their type (e.g., Int(1) < Int(2), String("a") < String("b")). Test NaN and -0.0 ordering consistency. Place tests in value.rs #[cfg(test)] module.; Implement PartialOrd and Ord for Value. Assign type-tag discriminant ordering: Undef=0, Bool=1, Int=2, Real=3, Scalar=4, String=5, Enum=6, List=7, Set=8, Map=9, Option=10. For f64 comparisons, use to_bits() to get total order (consistent with existing PartialEq). For now, only handle existing 6 variants; new variants will be added as they're created. This makes existing tests pass.; Write failing tests for Value::Enum variant: construct Value::Enum { type_name: "Color".into(), variant: "Red".into() }, verify Debug output, test equality of same vs different enum values, test ordering (Enum sorts after String, alphabetical within), test content_hash determinism and distinctness.; Add Value::Enum { type_name: String, variant: String } variant. Update all exhaustive matches in value.rs: content_hash (tag 6, hash type_name then variant), PartialEq::eq (both fields equal), PartialOrd/Ord (by type_name then variant). No Display on Value currently, so nothing to update there.; Write failing tests for Value::List variant: construct Value::List(vec![Value::Int(1), Value::Int(2)]), test equality, test empty list, test nested lists, test ordering (lexicographic on elements), test content_hash determinism.; Add Value::List(Vec<Value>) variant. Update all exhaustive matches in value.rs: content_hash (tag 7, combine hashes of all elements with length prefix), PartialEq::eq (delegate to Vec PartialEq), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic).; Write failing tests for Value::Set variant: construct from BTreeSet<Value>, test insert/contains operations, test that ordering is maintained, test equality of sets with same elements, test content_hash. Requires Ord on Value to compile.; Add Value::Set(BTreeSet<Value>) variant. Add `use std::collections::BTreeSet` to value.rs. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 8, sorted iteration over elements), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic on sorted elements).; Write failing tests for Value::Map variant: construct from BTreeMap<Value, Value>, test insert/get, test equality, test content_hash determinism, test ordering.; Add Value::Map(BTreeMap<Value, Value>) variant. Add `use std::collections::BTreeMap` to value.rs. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 9, iterate key-value pairs), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic on entries).; Write failing tests for Value::Option variant: construct Some and None variants, test equality, test content_hash (Some(x) != None, Some(x) != Some(y)), test ordering (None < Some(anything)).; Add Value::Option(Option<Box<Value>>) variant. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 10, None vs Some + inner hash), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (None < Some, then by inner value).; Write failing tests for Display on new Value variants: Enum shows "Color::Red", List shows "[1, 2, 3]", Set shows "{1, 2, 3}", Map shows "{\"a\": 1}", Option(None) shows "none", Option(Some(x)) shows "some(x)". Note: Value has no Display currently, but the task says 'update Display' — actually we need to add it or verify Debug. Check: LSP format_value is the display path. Test format_value for each new variant.; Update format_value in reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs to handle new Value variants: Enum, List, Set, Map, Option. This fixes the exhaustive match compilation error.; Write failing tests for new Type variants: construct Type::Enum("Color".into()), Type::List(Box::new(Type::Int)), Type::Set(Box::new(Type::String)), Type::Map(Box::new(Type::String), Box::new(Type::Real)), Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Int)), Type::Function { params: vec![Type::Int], return_type: Box::new(Type::Real) }. Test Display output: "Enum(Color)", "List<Int>", "Set<String>", "Map<String, Real>", "Option<Int>", "Function(Int) -> Real".; Add new Type variants: Enum(String), List(Box<Type>), Set(Box<Type>), Map(Box<Type>, Box<Type>), Option(Box<Type>), Function { params: Vec<Type>, return_type: Box<Type> }. Update Display::fmt exhaustive match with appropriate formatting for each. Update is_numeric to return false for all new variants (they're non-numeric).; Write failing tests for EnumDef: construct with name and variants, verify field access, test variant lookup helper. Write in tests/m5_types.rs.; Add EnumDef { name: String, variants: Vec<String> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives. Add a contains_variant(&str) -> bool helper method.; Write failing tests for PortDirection enum: construct In, Out, Bidi variants, verify Debug, Clone, PartialEq. Write in tests/m5_types.rs.; Add PortDirection enum { In, Out, Bidi } in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash derives.; Write failing tests for TraitRef and TraitBound: construct TraitRef with name and type_args, construct TraitBound wrapping a TraitRef. Verify field access and equality.; Add TraitRef { name: String, type_args: Vec<Type> } and TraitBound { trait_ref: TraitRef } structs in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives.; Write failing tests for TypeParam: construct with name, bounds, and optional default type. Verify field access.; Add TypeParam { name: String, bounds: Vec<TraitBound>, default: Option<Type> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives.; Write failing tests for TraitMember enum: construct each variant (Param, Port, Sub, Let, Constraint, AssociatedType), verify Debug and pattern matching.; Add TraitMember enum with variants: Param { name: String, ty: Type, default: Option<Value> }, Port { name: String, ty: Type, direction: PortDirection }, Sub { name: String, trait_ref: TraitRef }, Let { name: String, ty: Type, expr: String }, Constraint { expr: String }, AssociatedType { name: String, default: Option<Type> }. Derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq.; Write failing tests for TraitDef: construct a full trait definition with type_params, refinements, and members of various kinds. Verify field access and equality.; Add TraitDef { name: String, type_params: Vec<TypeParam>, refinements: Vec<String>, members: Vec<TraitMember> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq derives.; Write failing integration test in tests/exports.rs verifying all new M5 types are exported from the crate root: Value::Enum, Value::List, Value::Set, Value::Map, Value::Option, Type::Enum, Type::List, Type::Set, Type::Map, Type::Option, Type::Function, EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection.; Update lib.rs to add `pub mod traits;` and re-export all new types: EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection from traits module. Ensure the integration export test passes.

All 1 prerequisite and 7 steps completed. Added Display impl for Value enum covering all 11 variants (Bool, Int, Real, String, Scalar, Enum, List, Set, Map, Option, Undef). Added 12 unit tests in value.rs (primitives, enum, collections, option, nested) and 1 integration test in tests/m5_types.rs exercising Display through the public API. Total: 163 tests pass (144 unit + 2 exports + 17 m5_types), 0 failures, 0 regressions.

Verify all 72 existing tests pass (cargo test -p reify-types). Confirms the prior implementation is solid before adding Display.; Write failing tests for Value::Display for primitive variants in value.rs #[cfg(test)]: Bool(true) -> 'true', Bool(false) -> 'false', Int(42) -> '42', Real(3.14) -> '3.14', String("hello") -> '"hello"', Undef -> 'undef'. For Scalar, format as '{si_value} {dimension}' using DimensionVector's existing Display (e.g., Value::length(0.08) -> '0.08 m'). These tests will fail to compile since Value has no Display impl.; Implement `impl std::fmt::Display for Value` covering all 11 variants: Bool -> true/false, Int -> number, Real -> decimal, String -> double-quoted, Scalar -> '{si_value} {dimension}', Enum -> '{type_name}::{variant}', List -> '[elem1, elem2, ...]', Set -> '{elem1, elem2, ...}' (note: use set notation), Map -> '{key1: val1, key2: val2, ...}', Option(None) -> 'None', Option(Some(v)) -> 'Some({v})', Undef -> 'undef'. This makes step-1 tests pass.; Write tests for Value::Display for Enum variant: Value::Enum { type_name: 'Color', variant: 'Red' } displays as 'Color::Red'.; Write tests for Value::Display for collection variants: List([1,2,3]) -> '[1, 2, 3]', empty List -> '[]', Set({1,2}) -> '{1, 2}', empty Set -> '{}', Map({"a":1}) -> '{"a": 1}', empty Map -> '{}'.; Write tests for Value::Display for Option variant: Option(None) -> 'None', Option(Some(Int(42))) -> 'Some(42)'. Also test nested Display: List containing Option and Enum values.; Write integration test in tests/m5_types.rs that exercises Display through the public API: create each Value variant and assert format!("{}", value) produces expected output. This provides coverage from an external-crate perspective.; Final verification: run full test suite (cargo test -p reify-types). Ensure all 72+ existing tests still pass plus all new Display tests. No implementation changes expected — just verification and any minor fixups.

All 4 remaining post-review robustness fixes completed. Steps 8-10 were already committed from a prior iteration (305523c, 68e8215, bba210e) but plan.json wasn't updated. Step-11 implementation (literal() panic for M5 types) was staged but uncommitted — committed as 58ae608. Fix 1: Added Eq derive to TraitMember and TraitDef for consistency with all constituent types. Fix 2: literal() now panics explicitly for M5 Value variants (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option) instead of silently returning Type::Bool. All 163 reify-types tests + 19 reify-test-support tests pass. This completes all 11 steps of the plan.

All 2 prerequisites and 20 steps completed. End-to-end enum support: (1) Grammar: added enum_declaration rule to tree-sitter grammar, regenerated parser. (2) Parser: added EnumDecl struct, Declaration::Enum variant, ExprKind::EnumAccess variant. Two-pass lowering collects enum names first, then disambiguates member_access vs EnumAccess during expression lowering. (3) Compiler: added enum_defs field to CompiledModule, threaded enum_defs through compile_structure and compile_expr. EnumAccess compiles to Literal(Value::Enum) with variant validation. (4) Evaluator: added Value::Enum match arms in eval_eq (same type equality, cross-type false, enum vs non-enum false) and eval_cmp (returns Undef). Added 11 new tests across expr, syntax, and compiler crates including E2E pipeline test. All workspace tests pass with 0 failures.

All 4 prerequisites and 32 steps completed. (1) Grammar: replaced string-literal import with dot-path syntax supporting module, entity, destructured, aliased, entity-aliased, and pub import forms. Added import_path and import_items rules, regenerated parser. (2) Syntax: added ImportKind enum (Module, Entity, Destructured, Aliased, EntityAliased) and is_pub/content_hash fields to ImportDecl. Updated lower_import with uppercase-first heuristic for entity detection. (3) Compiler: created module_dag.rs with ModuleResolver (dot-to-path, std.*→stdlib/, file.ri then mod.ri fallback), ModuleDag (DFS with cycle detection via in-progress HashSet, deduplication via visited HashMap, post-order topological sort), and compile_project() entry point. (4) Updated 3 existing tests to use dot-path syntax. Escalated 1 broken test in reify-eval (outside scope). Added 24 new tests: 10 parser tests (all import forms), 6 resolve tests (std, local, nested, missing, mod.ri, file precedence), 8 DAG tests (circular, diamond, topo order, entity resolution, shadowing, re-export, backward compat). All 91 tests pass across reify-syntax and reify-compiler.

Update grammar.js: Replace string_literal-based import_declaration with dot-path syntax supporting all import forms: `import a.b.c`, `import a.b.C` (entity), `import a.b.{C, D}` (destructured), `import a.b as x` (aliased), `import a.b.C as X` (entity aliased), `pub import a.b.C` (re-export). Add `import_path` rule as dot-separated identifiers, `import_items` for braces, and `as` alias support. Run `tree-sitter generate` to regenerate parser.c, grammar.json, node-types.json.; Extend ImportDecl in reify-syntax/src/lib.rs: Add `ImportKind` enum (Module, Entity(String), Destructured(Vec<String>), Aliased { alias: String }, EntityAliased { entity: String, alias: String }) and `is_pub: bool` field to ImportDecl. Also add `content_hash: ContentHash` field for consistency with other decls.; Update ts_parser.rs `lower_import` to handle new grammar nodes: parse the dot-separated import_path, detect braces for destructured imports, detect `as` for aliases, detect `pub` keyword. Produce the correct ImportKind variant.; Create crates/reify-compiler/src/module_dag.rs skeleton: Define `ModuleDag` struct with `HashMap<String, CompiledModule>` for visited modules, a `Vec<String>` for topological order, and `HashSet<String>` for in-progress stack (cycle detection). Define `ModuleResolver` struct for path resolution with project_root and stdlib_root. Add `mod module_dag;` to compiler/lib.rs.; Write parser tests for basic dot-path import: `import std.math` parses to ImportDecl with path='std.math', kind=ImportKind::Module, is_pub=false. Test in crates/reify-syntax/tests/import_tests.rs.; Ensure grammar + ts_parser correctly parse `import std.math` into ImportDecl { path: 'std.math', kind: Module, is_pub: false }. Verify test from step-1 passes.; Write parser test for entity import: `import std.math.sqrt` parses to ImportDecl with kind=ImportKind::Entity('sqrt'). Test that the module path portion is 'std.math' and entity is 'sqrt'.; Implement entity import parsing in lower_import. When grammar produces an entity suffix node (final identifier after the last dot, with entity marker or context), produce ImportKind::Entity.; Write parser test for destructured import: `import std.mech.{Bolt, Nut}` parses to ImportDecl with kind=ImportKind::Destructured(vec!['Bolt', 'Nut']).; Implement destructured import parsing in grammar and ts_parser. Handle brace-enclosed comma-separated identifiers after the dot-path.; Write parser test for aliased module import: `import std.mech as m` parses to ImportDecl with kind=ImportKind::Aliased { alias: 'm' }.; Implement aliased import parsing. Detect `as` keyword after the import path in grammar and produce Aliased variant in ts_parser.; Write parser test for entity alias: `import std.mech.Bolt as StdBolt` parses to ImportDecl with kind=ImportKind::EntityAliased { entity: 'Bolt', alias: 'StdBolt' }.; Implement entity aliased import parsing. When `as` follows an entity import, produce EntityAliased variant.; Write parser test for pub import (re-export): `pub import internal.Helper` parses to ImportDecl with is_pub=true.; Implement pub import parsing. Detect `pub` keyword before `import` in grammar and set is_pub=true in ImportDecl.; Write compiler test for import path to filesystem resolution: `std.math` resolves to 'stdlib/math.ri' or 'stdlib/math/mod.ri'. `mylib.shapes` resolves to '<project_root>/mylib/shapes.ri'. Test in crates/reify-compiler/tests/import_resolve_tests.rs using temp directories with fixture .ri files.; Implement ModuleResolver::resolve_import_path(path: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, Diagnostic> in module_dag.rs. Convert dots to path separators. Check file.ri first, then dir/mod.ri. Handle std.* prefix mapping to stdlib/ directory.; Write compiler test for missing module: `import nonexistent.module` produces error diagnostic with clear message about module not found.; Implement missing module error handling in ModuleResolver: when neither file.ri nor dir/mod.ri exists, return descriptive error diagnostic.; Write compiler test for directory module resolution: `import std.mechanical.fasteners` finds stdlib/mechanical/fasteners/mod.ri when individual file stdlib/mechanical/fasteners.ri doesn't exist.; Ensure directory module fallback to mod.ri works correctly in resolve_import_path. Already part of step-14 logic but tested explicitly here.; Write compiler test for circular import detection: create temp files where module A imports B, B imports A. Compile A. Expect error diagnostic mentioning circular dependency. Test in crates/reify-compiler/tests/module_dag_tests.rs.; Implement cycle detection in ModuleDag::compile_module(): maintain an 'in-progress' HashSet during recursive DFS. When a module already in the set is encountered, emit circular dependency error with the cycle path.; Write compiler test for diamond import: create temp files A->B, A->C, B->D, C->D. Compile A. Assert D is compiled exactly once (check module count in DAG equals 4).; Implement deduplication in ModuleDag: track visited modules by canonical path in a HashMap. When a module has already been compiled, skip recompilation and return cached CompiledModule reference.; Write compiler test for topological ordering: create temp files A->B->C. Compile A. Assert the topological order is [C, B, A] (leaves first).; Implement topological sort in ModuleDag: record modules in post-order during DFS (after all dependencies are compiled). Return Vec<CompiledModule> in dependency order.; Write compiler test for name resolution with entity import: create two temp modules where module A defines `pub structure Bolt { param d: Scalar = 6mm }` and module B does `import A.Bolt` then `sub b = Bolt(d: 8mm)`. Compile B and verify Bolt resolves to A's definition (sub_component structure_name matches).; Implement import-based name resolution: after compiling imported modules, build a namespace map for the importing module. Entity imports add the entity name directly; Destructured imports add each listed name; Aliased imports add the alias as a qualified prefix. Wire this into the existing CompilationScope or a new ModuleScope layer.; Write compiler test for shadowing warning: module A defines `pub structure Bolt {...}`. Module B imports A.Bolt and also defines local `structure Bolt {...}`. Compile B and assert a warning diagnostic about shadowing is emitted, and the local Bolt definition is used.; Implement shadowing detection: when populating a module's namespace, if a local declaration name matches an imported name, emit a warning diagnostic but continue with the local definition taking precedence.; Write compiler test for re-export: module A defines `pub structure Helper {...}`. Module B does `pub import A.Helper`. Module C does `import B.Helper` then uses Helper. Compile C and verify Helper resolves through B to A's definition.; Implement re-export handling: when is_pub=true on an import in a compiled module, include the imported entity in the module's exported namespace. When other modules import from this module, they can see re-exported entities.; Write backward compatibility test: the canonical bracket.ri source (no imports, single module) compiles without errors through both the existing compile() function and the new compile_project() DAG entry point. Verify identical output.; Ensure backward compatibility: existing compile(parsed: &ParsedModule) function remains unchanged. Add compile_project(entry_path: &Path, resolver: &ModuleResolver) -> Result<Vec<CompiledModule>, Vec<Diagnostic>> as the multi-module entry point. Update CompiledImport struct to use the new ImportKind/is_pub fields. Ensure all existing tests still pass.
Debug fix for: Failures: tests failed, lint issues
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues

All 4 remaining post-review fix steps completed. Step-33: wrote test asserting CompiledImport preserves ImportKind and is_pub — failed as expected (fields missing). Step-34: added kind and is_pub fields to CompiledImport, updated compile() lowering and CompiledModuleBuilder — test passes. Step-35: wrote test proving in_progress cleanup failure — DAG is poisoned after parse error, second compile falsely reports circular dependency. Step-36: restructured compile_module() with inner closure pattern ensuring in_progress.remove() runs on all exit paths — test passes. All tests pass, clippy clean. This completes all 36 steps of the plan.
